channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: See How Houthis Put the Red Sea at Risk as an Alternative Oil Route
item.description: Saudi Arabia has diverted huge amounts of oil to the Red Sea since the Iran war began, but now the Iranian-backed Houthi militants say that they intend to block that route.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca F. Elliott, Lazaro Gamio and Samuel Granados
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: Iran’s Neighbors Long for a Deal, Any Deal, to End the War
item.description: Business owners in Pakistan are getting impatient for a chance to finally trade goods and oil along their long shared border with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier and Asim Hafeez
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Asim Hafeez for The New York Times

item.title: Stocks and the Economy Are Increasingly Relying on the A.I. Boom
item.description: Investment in artificial intelligence and related companies is lifting the stock market and spending across the economy.
item.dc:creator: Ben Casselman and Joe Rennison

item.title: Laredo’s Future Rides on Trump’s Next Trade Move
item.description: The economy of Laredo, Texas, has boomed under the president’s global tariffs. Now, his demands may threaten its success.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson
item.media:credit: Gabriel V. Cárdenas for The New York Times

item.title: Trump’s 50% Tariffs on Canada: What to Know, and What’s Next
item.description: The president said punishing 50 percent tariffs would begin by Aug. 19, a significant escalation of the trade war between Canada and the U.S. that he started over a year ago.
item.dc:creator: Ian Austen
item.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump and Mark Carney in the Oval Office last year.

item.title: Mamdani Calls Netanyahu a ‘War Criminal’ and Calls on U.S. to Arrest Him
item.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani challenged the United States to execute an international arrest warrant against Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel, which New York itself cannot do.
item.dc:creator: Liam Stack
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
item.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani said that New York cannot arrest Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel, but the United States should.

item.title: Coveted U.S. Nuclear Deal Is a Coup for Saudi Crown Prince
item.description: Saudi Arabia had for years sought the controversial deal to obtain American nuclear technology. It is expected to be sealed on Wednesday, deepening U.S.-Saudi ties for decades to come.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: Prince Mohammed bin Salman, the de facto Saudi ruler, and President Trump at the White House in November.

item.title: U.S.-Saudi Nuclear Deal Stokes Israeli Concerns of Mideast Arms Race
item.description: An Israeli minister sought to downplay the expected deal, while critics said it could spur other regional countries to pursue a nuclear program, and potentially the creation of a bomb.
item.dc:creator: Natan Odenheimer and Aaron Boxerman
item.media:credit: Jack Guez/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Nir Barkat, Israel’s minister of economy, in Tel Aviv last year. He has been the most senior Israeli government official to comment so far on the expected U.S.-Saudi nuclear deal.

item.title: How the Trump Administration’s Nuclear Deal With Saudi Arabia May Work
item.description: The Trump administration is expected to announce an agreement with Saudi Arabia on a civilian nuclear program. Such deals, known as 123 Agreements, are designed to prevent the development of weapons.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel
item.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump with Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman Al Saud in Washington last year.

item.title: He’s the Last Great Land Artist You’ve Never Heard Of
item.description: Charles Ross spent 50 years building “Star Axis,” a naked-eye observatory in New Mexico. Now his masterwork is ready. How to share it with a changed world?
item.dc:creator: Travis Diehl and Brad Trone
item.category: Los Angeles County Museum of Art
item.media:credit: Charles Ross/Artists Rights Society (ARS), New York
item.media:description: The Milky Way ribbons through the sky above “Star Axis.”

item.title: Donald Trump Jr.’s Investment Firm Posts Staggering Returns of 200 Percent
item.description: The fledgling firm, run by the president’s oldest son and his partner Omeed Malik, has made investments in A.I. and defense technology that have tripled in value in just months.
item.dc:creator: Maureen Farrell
item.media:credit: Chandan Khanna/AFP, via Getty Images (Malik); Alex Brandon/AP (Trump)
item.media:description: Omeed Malik, left, and Donald Trump Jr.

item.title: Women Are Too Controlling, She Wrote. Her Message Still Resonates.
item.description: The message in “The Surrendered Wife,” the best-selling book by Laura Doyle, was provocative. Many women credit it with saving their marriages.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Yee and Isadora Kosofsky
item.media:description: Laura Doyle, author of “The Surrendered Wife" and several other books, alongside her husband, John Doyle.

item.title: Trump Administration Pressures Some Immigrants to Self-Deport With Fines Up to $1.8 Million
item.description: Tens of thousands of immigrants have received letters to pay up or leave, as the government enlists debt collectors and seizes tax refunds.
item.dc:creator: Miriam Jordan
item.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
item.media:description: Immigrants waiting in line for hearings at Annandale Immigration Court in Annandale, Va., in May.

item.title: House Republicans Have Tied the Defense Policy Bill to Trump’s SAVE Act
item.description: House Republicans have tied their entire legislative agenda to President Trump’s voting restriction measure, as he has demanded.
item.dc:creator: Michael Gold
item.media:credit: Demetrius Freeman for The New York Times
item.media:description: Speaker Mike Johnson, pictured with other House Republican leaders at a news conference in June, has acted to placate Republican colleagues enraged that the SAVE America Act ran aground in the Senate.

item.title: Rubio and Chinese Diplomat Make Plans for Trump and Xi’s Washington Summit
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio spent much of a meeting with China’s foreign minister discussing the summit, but did not bring up President Trump’s claims of election interference by China.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Donald Trump with Xi Jinping in Beijing in May.

item.title: South Carolina and Nevada Fight to Kick Off Democrats’ 2028 Presidential Calendar
item.description: The calendar order could be hugely important to the 2028 presidential primary race. Democratic insiders will try to sketch out a schedule this week, and rival states are sniping at one another.
item.dc:creator: Shane Goldmacher
item.media:credit: Travis Dove for The New York Times; Caroline Brehman/EPA, via Shutterstock

item.title: Arizona Elections: 6 Takeaways From the Trump-Dominated Primaries
item.description: Republicans nominated a former N.F.L. kicker in a top House battleground, a Trump loyalist for governor and an election denier for the state’s top election post.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.media:credit: Adriana Zehbrauskas for The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Andy Biggs of Arizona, who was endorsed by President Trump, easily won the Republican primary for governor over Representative David Schweikert.

item.title: OpenAI Sued Over ChatGPT’s ‘Dangerous’ Health Advice
item.description: The case appears to be the first to argue that a chatbot’s advice harmed someone seeking guidance about a medical condition.
item.dc:creator: Teddy Rosenbluth
item.media:credit: Kiichiro Sato/Associated Press

item.title: With 2 Hires, Mamdani Blends Economic Development With Economic Justice
item.description: Anthony E. Shorris, a former deputy mayor and a partner at McKinsey, and Lina Khan, the former chair of the Federal Trade Commission, will lead the city’s Economic Development Corporation.
item.dc:creator: Jeffery C. Mays
item.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani said that in New York City “economic development and economic justice must go hand in hand.”

item.title: At a Utah Campus, an Emotional Odyssey After Charlie Kirk’s Death
item.description: Since Mr. Kirk was gunned down at Utah Valley University, the school and its students have struggled to find a way forward.
item.dc:creator: Robert Draper
item.media:credit: Kim Raff for The New York Times
item.media:description: From left, Caleb Chilcutt, Sage Lloyd and Sophie Grizzle, members of Utah Valley University’s chapter of Turning Point USA, in the campus courtyard where Charlie Kirk was gunned down in September.

item.title: A Hiker Confronts His Fear of Heights at Ausable Chasm in Upstate N.Y.
item.description: A jittery hiker signed up for a via ferrata tour on the cliffs of an Adirondacks gorge. Here’s what he learned on the ledges, rungs and exposed bridges.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hippensteel and Oliver Parini
item.media:description: One of the course’s suspension bridges, high above the Ausable River.

item.title: Gen Z-ers Are Ditching Sex. They Might Be On to Something.
item.description: Is celibacy cool now?
item.dc:creator: Nadja Spiegelman, Christine Emba, Carter Sherman and Vishakha Darbha
item.media:credit: illustration by The New York Times, photo by Peter Dazeley/Getty

item.title: Trump’s New Insult Won’t Save Republicans
item.description: The president’s attempt to drum up a third red scare is certain to fail.
item.dc:creator: Jamelle Bouie
item.media:credit: Aleksey Kondratyev for The New York Times

item.title: Dirty Lettuce
item.description: We look into the cyclospora outbreak.
item.dc:creator: Sam Sifton
item.media:credit: Claire Merchlinsky/The New York Times; Photographs via Getty

item.title: Louvre Gallery Reopens After Brazen Jewel Heist
item.description: Thieves stole crown jewels from the Apollo Gallery at the Louvre Museum in October, forcing its closure. Tourists finally returned on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Catherine Porter
item.media:credit: Pool photo by Lou Benoist
item.media:description: Visitors arrive during the reopening to the public of the Apollo Gallery, at the Louvre museum in Paris on Wednesday.
